name: tape-lint
description: PreToolUse(Edit|Write) deny for `.tape` edits, implemented in hexa-lang (`_tape_lint.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run` from hooks.json — no Python, no shell shim). Three checks, all diff-aware (pre-existing violations grandfathered, only newly-introduced or worsened items block). (1) fields rule — `@D` blocks accept only `do` / `dont`; new `why` · `tool` · `note` · `ref` · `ex` · ... refused. Fires on any `*.tape` path. (2) length cap — `do` / `dont` value content > 100 chars refused (split overflow into multiple do/dont or distinct @D blocks). commons.tape + project.tape only. (3) authoring-language — when `sidecar prefs` `code` axis is `english`, lines newly introducing non-Latin scripts (Hangul · CJK · Hiragana · Katakana) are refused. Pref is read at runtime so `/prefs code <lang>` reshapes the rule. commons.tape + project.tape only. NO opt-out by design — no env var, no config file, no exception list.
